segments.text: When Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, so that he could not see, he called Esau his elder son, and said to him,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: My son?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He said to him,
Here I am.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: See now, I am old. I don’t know the day of my death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now therefore, please take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field, and get me venison.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Make me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat, and that my soul may bless you before I die.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Rebekah heard when Isaac spoke to Esau his son. Esau went to the field to hunt for venison, and to bring it.
Rebekah spoke to Jacob her son, saying,
Behold, I heard your father speak to Esau your brother, saying,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: ‘Bring me venison, and make me savory food, that I may eat, and bless you before Yahweh before my death.’
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command you.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Go now to the flock and get me two good young goats from there. I will make them savory food for your father, such as he loves.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: You shall bring it to your father, that he may eat, so that he may bless you before his death.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Jacob said to Rebekah his mother,
Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: What if my father touches me? I will seem to him as a deceiver, and I would bring a curse on myself, and not a blessing.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: His mother said to him,
Let your curse be on me, my son. Only obey my voice, and go get them for me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He went, and got them, and brought them to his mother. His mother made savory food, such as his father loved.
Rebekah took the good clothes of Esau, her elder son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob, her younger son.
She put the skins of the young goats on his hands, and on the smooth of his neck.
She gave the savory food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
He came to his father, and said,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: My father?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He said,
Here I am. Who are you, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Jacob said to his father,
I am Esau your firstborn. I have done what you asked me to do. Please arise, sit and eat of my venison, that your soul may bless me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Isaac said to his son,
How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: He said,
Because Yahweh your God gave me success.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Isaac said to Jacob,
Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Jacob went near to Isaac his father. He felt him, and said,
The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: He didn’t recognize him, because his hands were hairy, like his brother Esau’s hands. So he blessed him.
He said,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Are you really my son Esau?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He said,
I am.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son’s venison, that my soul may bless you.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: He brought it near to him, and he ate. He brought him wine, and he drank.
His father Isaac said to him,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Come near now, and kiss me, my son.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He came near, and kissed him. He smelled the smell of his clothing, and blessed him, and said,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Behold, the smell of my sonis as the smell of a field which Yahweh has blessed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
God give you of the dew of the sky, of the fatness of the earth, 
and plenty of grain and new wine.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers. Let your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you. Blessed be everyone who blesses you.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had just gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
He also made savory food, and brought it to his father. He said to his father,
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Let my father arise, and eat of his son’s venison, that your soul may bless me.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Isaac his father said to him,
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Isaac his father

segments.text: He said,
I am your son, your firstborn, Esau.
segments.speaker: Isaac his father

segments.text: Isaac trembled violently, and said,
Who, then, is he who has taken venison, and brought it to me, and I have eaten of all before you came, and have blessed him? Yes, he will be blessed.
segments.speaker: Isaac trembled violently, and

segments.text: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father,
Bless me, even me also, my father.
segments.speaker: Isaac trembled violently, and

segments.text: He said,
Your brother came with deceit, and has taken away your blessing.
segments.speaker: Isaac trembled violently, and

segments.text: He said,
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Isn’t he rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright. See, now he has taken away my blessing.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: He said,
Haven’t you reserved a blessing for me?
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Isaac answered Esau,
Behold, I have made him your lord, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants. I have sustained him with grain and new wine. What then will I do for you, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Esau said to his father,
Do you have just one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, my father.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Esau lifted up his voice, and wept.
Isaac his father answered him,

Behold, your dwelling will be of the fatness of the earth, 
and of the dew of the sky from above.
segments.speaker: Isaac his father

segments.text:
You will live by your sword, and you will serve your brother. 
It will happen, when you will break loose, 
that you will shake his yoke from off your neck.
segments.speaker: Isaac his father

segments.text: Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father blessed him. Esau said in his heart,
The days of mourning for my father are at hand. Then I will kill my brother Jacob.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: The words of Esau, her elder son, were told to Rebekah. She sent and called Jacob, her younger son, and said to him,
Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself about you by planning to kill you.
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Laban, my brother, in Haran.
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: Stay with him a few days, until your brother’s fury turns away—
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: until your brother’s anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send, and get you from there. Why should I be bereaved of you both in one day?
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: Rebekah said to Isaac,
I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob takes a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these, of the daughters of the land, what good will my life do me?
segments.speaker: Rebekah
